sections.heading: What interviewers assess
sections.content: They want evidence that you can learn an unfamiliar stack without pretending to know everything.

sections.heading: STAR structure
sections.content: Situation: moving from Java EE toward Spring Boot and Angular. Task: become productive quickly. Action: map familiar concepts, build a working project, study gaps and request feedback. Result: demonstrate a deployable application and explain transferable knowledge.

sections.heading: Strong answer
sections.content: I am transparent about what I know and what I am still learning. I reduce the gap by connecting new concepts to systems I have already built, then validate the learning with code rather than only courses.

sections.heading: What interviewers assess
sections.content: They look for professional disagreement, listening, evidence-based decisions and preservation of trust.

sections.heading: STAR structure
sections.content: Describe a technical disagreement, the risk involved, how you gathered data, invited the other perspective and reached a decision.

sections.heading: Strong answer
sections.content: I separate the person from the problem. I make assumptions visible, use production evidence or a small experiment, and document the decision so the team can revisit it if conditions change.

sections.heading: Leadership without title
sections.content: Leadership means improving outcomes beyond your assigned ticket: identifying systemic problems, helping colleagues and making future work safer.

sections.heading: Your evidence
sections.content: Use examples such as eliminating an N+1 problem, introducing observability, improving test standards or clarifying an ambiguous requirement before implementation.

sections.heading: Strong answer
sections.content: I lead by making the problem understandable, proposing a practical path, involving the people affected and leaving documentation or automation so the improvement survives me.

sections.heading: What interviewers assess
sections.content: They want prioritization and calm execution, not heroic overtime.

sections.heading: Response framework
sections.content: Stabilize the incident, protect data, communicate impact, reduce scope, assign ownership, monitor recovery and perform a blameless retrospective.

sections.heading: Strong answer
sections.content: Under pressure I first establish facts and business impact. I avoid changing several variables at once, keep stakeholders updated and prefer a safe rollback or containment before a risky permanent fix.

sections.heading: Why REWE Digital
sections.content: Connect your motivation to owning products used by REWE Group, solving real logistics problems, working in an international team and modernizing enterprise platforms.

sections.heading: Why TRAB
sections.content: TRAB combines Java, Spring Boot, Angular, messaging, databases and operational logistics. It matches your enterprise background while offering clear growth in the newer stack.

sections.heading: Strong answer
sections.content: I am motivated by systems where technical quality has visible operational impact. Transport logistics is exactly that: reliability and performance affect warehouses, drivers, planners and thousands of stores.
